claim: Longitudinal MC evidence maps become safer when missing data propagates as explicit uncertainty instead of being interpreted as absence, improvement, stability, cure, discovery, or resolved risk.
revision_reason: Adds a gate for longitudinal records where gaps can otherwise be misread as improvement, stability, or proof.
testability: Validator passes only when every longitudinal variable carries an explicit missingness state and every derived inference propagates that state into uncertainty, blocked promotion, or a safe next action.
falsification_route: If a packet can infer absence, improvement, cure, discovery, stability, or resolved risk from not_asked, not_recorded, withheld_for_privacy, or unknown_source_quality fields, the hypothesis fails.

records.revision_reason: Separate observed values from unknown values before trend inference.
records.falsification_route: Would fail if any not_recorded variable is interpreted as symptom absent, risk resolved, cure, diagnosis, or discovery proof.

derived_inference.text: Sleep duration was observed for this record; posture-linked heart-rate and context-stress variables are unavailable, so cross-record autonomic/context pattern inference remains blocked.

records.next_executable_action: Collect the same variable set in the next record or mark each unavailable field with a reason-coded missingness state.

records.revision_reason: Negative control checks that missing values cannot be treated as improvement.
records.falsification_route: This record should fail because it interprets not_recorded data as resolved.

derived_inference.text: Because no eye-pressure note was recorded, the issue appears resolved.

records.next_executable_action: Do not promote; revise into vet-facing question-prep with explicit unknown state.
